title: Public Interest Anti-Corruption Charter
mission: Expose wrongdoing, help humanity, protect victims, follow the documents, and map concentrated power for the public good.
publicLine: A lawful public-interest resource for exposing corruption, institutional abuse, secrecy, trafficking networks, financial influence, policy capture, and power concentration through evidence, not noise.
principles: Protect victims, witnesses, children, private citizens, and vulnerable people.
Follow documents, dates, money, institutions, contracts, court records, public filings, policy mandates, and source-linked reporting.
Separate fact, allegation, pattern, hypothesis, speculation, and debunked claim.
Use strong public-interest wording without turning association, rumour, symbolism, or internet lore into proof.
Track corruption as systems: incentives, institutions, money routes, legal failures, secrecy, procurement, regulation, influence, and enforcement gaps.
Make the site useful to ordinary people: explain what happened, who benefits, what records exist, what is missing, and what to watch next.
readerPromise: The site will not ask the reader to believe first. It will show the lane, the record, the evidence class, the boundary, and the next source route.
hardBoundaries: No direct accusation against private people without source-grade evidence.
No blaming populations, races, religions, or ordinary groups for institutional wrongdoing.
